MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-30-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR AND AGAINST DEFENDANT NANCY MAXSON IN THE AMOUNT OF $20,351.94 PLUS COSTS OF $985.50. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH COMPLIES WITH CCP 437C(G) AND STATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT OPPOSED AND A PROPOSED JUDGMENT THAT CONFORMS TO THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM; MTORREANO@SFTC.ORG. [302]
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 61703761) FILED BY PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 2.0 HOURS

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAR-14-2018 CONTINUED TO MAY-16-2018 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR PLAINTIFF TO FILE ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT & JUDGMENT THEREON. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
ORDER AFTER HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT MAXSON, NANCY MULLANE $20,351.94 PRINCIPAL, $985.50 COSTS, TOTAL JUDGMENT $21,337.44, SEE SCANNED DOC
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAY-16-2018 IS OFF CALENDAR. JUDGMENT ON FILE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-27-2018 Continued To May-16-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-30-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance by either party, or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication as follows:

Plaintiff American Express Centurion Bank's motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiff is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendant Nancy Maxson in the amount of $20,351.94 plus costs of $985.50.

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and states that the motion was not opposed and a proposed judgment that conforms to the Court's ruling. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-27-2017 Continued To Mar-14-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
